---
title: "Baselines"
subtitle: "What classical and multivariate methods achieve across three countries"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
metrics = FileAttachment("_data/metrics.json").json()
Plot = import("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/plot@0.6/+esm")
```

## Country

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedCountry = Inputs.radio(
  ["norway", "canada", "sweden"],
  {label: "Country", value: "norway",
   format: v => ({"norway": "Norway", "canada": "Canada", "sweden": "Sweden"})[v]}
)
```

We evaluate 9 classical/multivariate baselines plus Chronos-2 zero-shot on the **validation era** (2006--2015, 120 monthly rolling forecast origins):

| Category | Methods |
|----------|---------|
| **Naive** | Random Walk, Seasonal Naive |
| **Univariate** | AR(p), ARIMA, ETS |
| **Multivariate** | VAR, BVAR (Minnesota), Factor Model |
| **ML** | Elastic Net |
| **Foundation** | Chronos-2 (zero-shot) |


## Performance comparison (MASE)

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedMetric = Inputs.radio(
  ["mase", "rmse"],
  {label: "Metric", value: "mase"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-comparison
//| fig-cap: "Average performance across target variables by method and horizon. Lower is better."

{
  const valMetrics = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === selectedCountry && d.era === "validation"
  );

  // Compute average across targets per method/horizon
  const grouped = {};
  for (const d of valMetrics) {
    const key = `${d.method}_${d.horizon}`;
    if (!grouped[key]) grouped[key] = {method: d.display_name, horizon: d.horizon, category: d.category, vals: []};
    const v = d[selectedMetric];
    if (v != null) grouped[key].vals.push(v);
  }

  const data = Object.values(grouped)
    .filter(g => g.vals.length > 0)
    .map(g => ({...g, value: g.vals.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0) / g.vals.length}));

  // Filter out methods with extreme values (VAR/Factor can blow up)
  const maxY = selectedMetric === "mase" ? 2.5 : null;
  const filtered = maxY ? data.filter(d => d.value < maxY) : data;

  const categoryColors = {
    "naive": "#95a5a6",
    "classical": "#2ecc71",
    "multivariate": "#3498db",
    "ml": "#f39c12",
    "foundation": "#9b59b6"
  };

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 420,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {
      label: "Forecast horizon (months)",
      ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]
    },
    y: {
      label: selectedMetric.toUpperCase(),
      grid: true
    },
    color: {
      domain: filtered.map(d => d.method),
      range: filtered.map(d => categoryColors[d.category] || "#999")
    },
    marks: [
      selectedMetric === "mase" ? Plot.ruleY([1.0], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "6,4"}) : null,
      Plot.line(filtered, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(filtered, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", fill: "method",
        r: 4, tip: true
      })
    ].filter(Boolean)
  });
}
```

**Color key:** [Naive]{style="color:#95a5a6;font-weight:bold"} | [Univariate]{style="color:#2ecc71;font-weight:bold"} | [Multivariate]{style="color:#3498db;font-weight:bold"} | [ML]{style="color:#f39c12;font-weight:bold"} | [Foundation]{style="color:#9b59b6;font-weight:bold"}. MASE < 1 (dashed line) beats the random walk.


## Summary table

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const valMetrics = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === selectedCountry && d.era === "validation"
  );

  // Get unique methods
  const methodOrder = ["random_walk", "seasonal_naive", "ar", "arima", "ets", "var", "bvar", "factor", "elastic_net", "zero_shot"];
  const methodSet = new Set(valMetrics.map(d => d.method));
  const methods = methodOrder.filter(m => methodSet.has(m));

  const rows = methods.map(method => {
    const mData = valMetrics.filter(d => d.method === method);
    const row = {Method: mData[0]?.display_name || method, Category: mData[0]?.category || ""};
    for (const h of [1, 3, 6, 12]) {
      const hVals = mData.filter(d => d.horizon === h).map(d => d[selectedMetric]).filter(v => v != null);
      const avg = hVals.length > 0 ? hVals.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0) / hVals.length : null;
      row[`h=${h}`] = avg != null ? avg.toFixed(3) : "---";
    }
    return row;
  });

  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    columns: ["Method", "Category", "h=1", "h=3", "h=6", "h=12"],
    width: {Method: 180, Category: 100}
  });
}
```


## Detailed results by variable

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
countryTargets = ({
  "norway": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  "canada": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  "sweden": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "unemployment"]
})

viewof selectedVariable = Inputs.select(
  countryTargets[selectedCountry],
  {label: "Target variable", value: "cpi", format: v => v.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase())}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-variable-detail
//| fig-cap: "Method comparison for the selected variable."

{
  const data = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === selectedCountry && d.era === "validation" && d.target === selectedVariable
  ).map(d => ({
    method: d.display_name,
    horizon: d.horizon,
    value: d[selectedMetric],
    category: d.category
  })).filter(d => d.value != null && d.value < (selectedMetric === "mase" ? 3 : Infinity));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {
      label: "Forecast horizon (months)",
      ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]
    },
    y: {
      label: selectedMetric.toUpperCase(),
      grid: true
    },
    marks: [
      selectedMetric === "mase" ? Plot.ruleY([1.0], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "6,4"}) : null,
      Plot.line(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", fill: "method",
        r: 4, tip: true
      })
    ].filter(Boolean)
  });
}
```


## Relative to random walk

How much does each method improve over the naive random walk baseline? A MASE below 1.0 means the method is better.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-relative
//| fig-cap: "Average MASE across target variables. Values below 1.0 (dashed line) beat the random walk."

{
  const valMetrics = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === selectedCountry && d.era === "validation" && d.method !== "random_walk"
  );

  const grouped = {};
  for (const d of valMetrics) {
    const key = `${d.method}_${d.horizon}`;
    if (!grouped[key]) grouped[key] = {method: d.display_name, horizon: d.horizon, vals: []};
    if (d.mase != null) grouped[key].vals.push(d.mase);
  }

  const data = Object.values(grouped)
    .filter(g => g.vals.length > 0)
    .map(g => ({...g, mase: g.vals.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0) / g.vals.length}))
    .filter(d => d.mase < 2.5);

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {
      label: "Forecast horizon (months)",
      ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]
    },
    y: {
      label: "MASE (< 1 = beats RW)",
      grid: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleY([1.0], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "6,4", strokeWidth: 1.5}),
      Plot.line(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "mase", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "mase", fill: "method",
        r: 4, tip: true
      })
    ]
  });
}
```
